summary: Drew DeVault's August 2021 status update reports progress from his new base in Amsterdam. The bulk of his work focused on his programming language project (Hare), where a major codegen rewrite was completed and merged after all tests passed. He also reports SourceHut progress, including shipping the lists.sr.ht GraphQL API and working on GraphQL-native webhooks. He outlines near-term plans for both projects, including language design questions and expanding GraphQL support across SourceHut services.
key_points: A major codegen rewrite for the Hare compiler was completed and merged, with all tests passing
The lists.sr.ht GraphQL API shipped, with plans for thread parsing and write operations
GraphQL-native webhooks are being designed, targeting meta.sr.ht for initial release
Near-term language work includes I/O abstraction improvements and finalizing design questions before the hosted compiler
money_quotes: I mentioned in the last update that we broke ground on a codegen rewrite, and yesterday all of our tests finally passed and I merged it.
The new design is much better, and we should be able to simplify it even further still when we write the hosted compiler in the near future.
This will also give us a better basis for a small number of experiments we'd like to do before finalizing the language design.
key_insight: Both Hare and SourceHut are advancing steadily, with a major compiler codegen rewrite landing and SourceHut's GraphQL API surface expanding toward full coverage.
